triage.incident_classification: Potential IDOR exploitation via invoice enumeration
initial_hypotheses: Automated invoice enumeration against invoice search endpoint.
Possible IDOR exploitation due to high invoice diversity per IP.
Likely bot-driven activity due to convergence of heuristic and anomaly signals.
investigation_plan: Review top risk IPs and their invoice enumeration pattern.
Validate whether accessed invoices belong to different users.
Review auth token reuse and suspicious user-agent patterns.
Correlate risk scoring with Isolation Forest anomaly results.
Prioritize containment for critical IPs with confirmed high-risk behavior.

forensic_analysis.attack_pattern_explanation: The evidence shows high-volume invoice access, high invoice diversity and elevated sequential access ratio. This combination is consistent with automated enumeration of direct object identifiers.

automation_assessment.rationale: Top IPs converge across risk scoring, bot likelihood and Isolation Forest anomaly detection.

idor_evidence_assessment.reason: Multiple IPs accessed thousands of unique invoice IDs with strong sequential patterns, suggesting enumeration of object references.

top_enumeration_pattern.idor_evidence: Sequential invoice enumeration pattern. unique_invoice_ids=6481, total_requests=12409, sequential_access_ratio=0.6628

mitre_attack_mapping_high_level.note: Mapped at high level due to identifier enumeration behavior.
Invoice data may represent sensitive business/user information.

mitre_attack_mapping_high_level.technique: Use of distributed IPs or repeated low-rate access
mitre_attack_mapping_high_level.note: Multiple suspicious IPs may indicate campaign distribution.

immediate.business_risk: Possible false positives may impact legitimate users behind shared IPs.
Low to medium; may affect heavy legitimate usage.
Low.

strategic.description: Ensure invoice access checks validate ownership/authorization server-side.
Review tokens observed in suspicious access patterns.
Add rules for sequential invoice enumeration patterns.

mini_playbook: Validate critical IPs against customer support and internal allowlists.
Apply dry-run blocklist simulation.
Enable rate limiting for invoice search endpoint.
Escalate object-level authorization fix to application security owners.
Review token exposure and revoke suspicious tokens if confirmed.
Monitor anomaly score and risk score deltas after containment.
response_recommendation.executive_summary: Incident classified as critical / P1. Likely IDOR enumeration confirmed by forensic analysis. Patient-zero candidate: 204.210.158.207.

actions_waiting_approval.business_risk: Possible false positives may impact legitimate users behind shared IPs.

triage.incident_classification: Potential IDOR exploitation via invoice enumeration
initial_hypotheses: Automated invoice enumeration against invoice search endpoint.
Possible IDOR exploitation due to high invoice diversity per IP.
Likely bot-driven activity due to convergence of heuristic and anomaly signals.
investigation_plan: Review top risk IPs and their invoice enumeration pattern.
Validate whether accessed invoices belong to different users.
Review auth token reuse and suspicious user-agent patterns.
Correlate risk scoring with Isolation Forest anomaly results.
Prioritize containment for critical IPs with confirmed high-risk behavior.

forensic_analysis.attack_pattern: Automated sequential invoice enumeration through exposed application object identifiers.
forensic_analysis.attack_pattern_explanation: The evidence shows high-volume invoice access, high invoice diversity and elevated sequential access ratio. This combination is consistent with automated enumeration of direct object identifiers.

automation_assessment.rationale: Top IPs converge across risk scoring, bot likelihood and Isolation Forest anomaly detection.

idor_evidence_assessment.reason: Multiple IPs accessed thousands of unique invoice IDs with strong sequential patterns, suggesting enumeration of object references.

top_enumeration_pattern.idor_evidence: Sequential invoice enumeration pattern. unique_invoice_ids=6481, total_requests=12409, sequential_access_ratio=0.6628

mitre_attack_mapping_high_level.note: Mapped at high level due to identifier enumeration behavior.
Invoice data may represent sensitive business/user information.

mitre_attack_mapping_high_level.technique: Use of distributed IPs or repeated low-rate access
mitre_attack_mapping_high_level.note: Multiple suspicious IPs may indicate campaign distribution.

mitre_attack_mapping.technique: Application endpoint and object identifier enumeration
mitre_attack_mapping.rationale: The attacker enumerates invoice-like objects through exposed application endpoints.
The observed behavior indicates large-scale access to invoice objects.
The investigation observed suspicious token reuse across invoice access patterns.

immediate.business_risk: Possible false positives may impact legitimate users behind shared IPs.
Low to medium; may affect heavy legitimate usage.
Low.

strategic.description: Ensure invoice access checks validate ownership/authorization server-side.
Review tokens observed in suspicious access patterns.
Add rules for sequential invoice enumeration patterns.

mini_playbook: Validate critical IPs against customer support and internal allowlists.
Apply dry-run blocklist simulation.
Enable rate limiting for invoice search endpoint.
Escalate object-level authorization fix to application security owners.
Review token exposure and revoke suspicious tokens if confirmed.
Monitor anomaly score and risk score deltas after containment.
response_recommendation.executive_summary: Incident classified as critical / P1. Likely IDOR enumeration confirmed by forensic analysis. Patient-zero candidate: 204.210.158.207.
